product_summary: Replace this summary after research. State the chosen user, workflow, wedge, and why this build should exist.

constitution.title: New Topic Build Constitution
constitution.what_it_is: Replace this with a precise product statement after research. Describe the user, the promise, and the core workflow in plain language.
core_loop: A user brings a real input into the system.
The system turns that input into reviewable structured state.
The product produces a concrete output or decision artifact the user can act on.
positioning: Replace this with the chosen wedge against direct competitors.
State what the product will not try to do in v1.

items.label: One narrow wedge
items.text: The MVP must stay focused on the workflow proved by research, not a broad platform imitation.

items.label: Reviewable outputs
items.text: Core outputs must remain inspectable and correctable by a human before final use.

items.label: Local-first buildability
items.text: A reviewer must be able to run the product locally without exotic infrastructure.

items.label: Traceable product claims
items.text: Do not encode capabilities or promises that are not backed by the research and the actual implementation.

primitives.name: Actor
primitives.summary: The primary user or operational role interacting with the product.

primitives.name: Source Record
primitives.summary: The incoming object, document, or trigger that starts the workflow.

primitives.name: Reviewable Draft
primitives.summary: An intermediate output that can be inspected, edited, and approved.

primitives.name: Exported Artifact
primitives.summary: The final object or output package the user leaves with.

file_ownership.description: Main implementation surface for the product.
Core entities, state transitions, and business rules.
The operator-facing flow and review surfaces.

done_criteria.name: Core Workflow
criteria: A first-time user can complete the happy path from input to final output.
The main product state is visible and reviewable.
The output matches the chosen wedge from research.

debt.title: New Topic Initial Compiler Work Items
debt.intro: > Replace these placeholders with product-specific work after research. The goal is to encode the chosen spec as execution waves, not a generic backlog.

work_items.title: Implement the core input-to-draft workflow
ground_truth: Replace with facts learned in research and the baseline repo state.
scope: Replace with the exact workflow to implement.
State what gets created, transformed, and approved.
acceptance: A real sample input can flow through the product.
The user can inspect or edit the intermediate state.
The result can be finalized without hidden manual steps.

work_items.title: Add verification, demo data, and export path
ground_truth: Replace with actual constraints from the chosen product wedge.
scope: Add demo or fixture data for the happy path.
Add the export or completion artifact the user expects.
Add tests or validation around the core workflow.
acceptance: A reviewer can demo the end-to-end flow locally.
The main output can be exported or completed visibly.
The product has at least one real verification gate.

review.title: Factory Verification Gates
review.intro: > Use these as generic starter gates, then tighten them for the product you actually chose.
how_to_use: Map each work item to a risk tier in factory.yaml.
Run the required gates before marking an item complete.
Escalate only when a human approval condition is explicitly met.

gates.title: Type Safety
gates.failure_action: Stop and return the errors to the implementing agent.
checks: Public types match the intended workflow.
No placeholder types remain in the changed surface.

gates.title: Happy Path Verification
gates.failure_action: Block merge until the main flow is working.
checks: Human review or approval step remains intact if required.

gates.title: Scope Discipline
gates.pass_condition: Change stays inside the chosen wedge
gates.failure_action: Cut scope or split into a later item.
checks: No unresearched product surface was added opportunistically.
The implementation still matches the research-backed wedge.

merge_protocol: Check the implementation against CLAUDE.md invariants.
Reject work that widens scope without a research-backed reason.

factory.description: Starter compiler config. Replace risk tiers and verification commands once the product is concrete.
